diagrams.title: Collections Framework Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the complete interface and class hierarchy of Java Collections Framework
elements: Map (separate hierarchy) → HashMap, LinkedHashMap, TreeMap, Hashtable
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the relationship between interfaces and implementations helps in choosing the right collection for specific use cases.

diagrams.title: HashMap Internal Structure
diagrams.description: Visual representation of how HashMap stores key-value pairs internally
elements: Each bucket contains linked list or tree
Node contains: hash, key, value, next
New bucket array twice the size, all entries rehashed
diagrams.purpose: Understanding hashing and bucket structure clarifies time complexity and helps diagnose performance issues.

diagrams.title: Iterator vs ListIterator
diagrams.description: Comparison of iterator capabilities and movement directions
elements: Iterator: hasNext(), next(), remove() - Forward only
ListIterator: + hasPrevious(), previous(), add(), set() - Bidirectional
Both support element removal during iteration
ListIterator knows index position, Iterator doesn't
ListIterator available only for List implementations
diagrams.purpose: Choosing the right iterator type affects ability to modify collections during traversal.
